stories.title: SpaceX to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly acquiring the AI coding platform Cursor.
The acquisition is valued at a massive $60 billion.
This signals a major strategic move by SpaceX into advanced AI development and software tooling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant release in the LLM space.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its use within the transformers and text-generation ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama indicates relevance to the ongoing large language model (LLM) ecosystem.

stories.title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: OpenAI is reportedly incurring multi-billion dollar annual losses.
The financial strain is attributed to R&D costs significantly outpacing revenue growth.
The leak of audited financial documents provides concrete insight into the operational economics of major AI labs.

stories.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability in Copilot allowed attackers to steal Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) codes.
The exploit highlights systemic failures in current industry practices regarding Large Language Model (LLM) security.
The incident serves as a major cautionary tale regarding the security posture of AI-integrated tools.

stories.title: SpaceX to buy Cursor for $60B
stories.author: itsmarcelg
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly planning to acquire Cursor.
The proposed acquisition valuation is extremely high, stated at $60 billion.
This signals a major strategic move by SpaceX into the AI/software development space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model responses and faster inference speeds.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Snap is finally about to ship AR glasses — and they cost a fortune
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Snap is launching its first consumer augmented reality (AR) glasses, named 'Specs'.
The device is positioned as a 'wearable computer' and has a high initial price tag of $2,195.
Preorders are available with a $200 deposit, with expected shipping in the US and UK this fall.

stories.title: FusionRS: A Large-Scale RGB-Infrared Remote Sensing Dataset for Dual-Modal Vision-Language Foundation Models
stories.author: Jiujiang Guo
bullet_points: Introduces FusionRS, a large-scale RGB-Infrared remote sensing dataset.
Addresses the gap in current remote sensing models which primarily focus on RGB imagery, neglecting infrared data.
Highlights that infrared data offers crucial, illumination-invariant features (like thermal intensity) to enhance vision-language learning.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the rising inference costs in long-horizon LLM agent sessions caused by context accumulation.
It solves a critical trade-off between text sparsity (pruning) and prompt cache continuity, which is often broken by existing mutation methods.
The framework uses a dual-granularity context management approach to maintain cache efficiency while managing context.

stories.title: ActiveSAM: Image-Conditional Class Pruning for Fast and Accurate Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: ActiveSAM addresses the inefficiency of using large models like SAM 3 for Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation (OVSS).
It introduces a training-free, zero-shot inference framework to convert SAM 3 into an active-vocabulary segmenter.
The core innovation involves pruning the decoding process to only focus on the small, active subset of classes present in each image.

stories.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
stories.author: Wenhai Sun
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that Differential Privacy (DP) inherently protects Federated Learning (FL) against backdoor attacks.
Demonstrates a fundamental tension where DP compliance masks the statistical signatures needed to detect malicious updates.
Suggests that while bypassing DP allows current defenses to work, adhering to DP can inadvertently enable attacks.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the problem where traditional context management (pruning/eviction) causes cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity approach to balance context sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
stories.author: Xingyu Fu
bullet_points: Introduces ContextRL, a new context-aware Reinforcement Learning (RL) method designed to enhance LLM performance.
Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: difficulty in identifying small, decisive pieces of evidence within long or complex contexts (e.g., tool traces or images).
ContextRL uses an 'indirect' auxiliary objective, supervising intermediate steps rather than just the final answer, to improve long-horizon and multimodal reasoning.

stories.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
stories.author: Qingyao Ai
bullet_points: Introduces MetaSyn, a new dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals.
The dataset is designed to benchmark LLM Agents on the complex, multi-stage process of scientific evidence synthesis (retrieval, selection, aggregation).
Addresses a gap in current benchmarks by providing ground truth across the entire pipeline of systematic scientific reasoning.

stories.title: TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: TokenPilot addresses the high inference costs associated with context accumulation in long-horizon LLM agent sessions.
It tackles the problem where traditional context management (pruning/eviction) causes cache invalidation due to sequence mutations.
The framework introduces a dual-granularity approach to balance text sparsity with prompt cache continuity.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard modern ML tooling.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: All the latest news on Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Android 17 introduces multitasking features like floating 'Bubble' app windows and a 50/50 split gaming mode for foldables.
Wear OS 7 focuses on improving smartwatch functionality with Live Updates and enhanced battery life.
The ecosystem is advancing towards new hardware with preparations for Android XR smart glasses.

stories.title: Google launches Wear OS 7 with Live Updates and a battery life boost
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google is rolling out Wear OS 7 updates for Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4.
The key new feature is 'Live Updates,' which tracks real-time events on the smartwatch.
The update also includes a reported battery life boost for Wear OS devices.

stories.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
stories.author: Victoria Song
bullet_points: Qualcomm is developing the Snapdragon Reality Elite chip to power next-generation XR devices.
The announcement highlights the ongoing development and advancement of the smart glasses category.
Hands-on experience with a device utilizing the new chip has already been made available.

stories.title: The Google / Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available to preorder
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: The Google/Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available for preorder.
The device is priced starting at $99 and is positioned as the second Android XR device.
A full launch across major markets (US, UK, Japan, etc.) is anticipated this Fall.

stories.title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The Pentagon is utilizing generative AI to draft reports required by Congress.
The Department of Defense reports that 1.5 million personnel are currently using generative AI tools.
The story highlights the integration of AI into high-level government and defense operations.

stories.title: Mobileye is entering the US robotaxi market with standalone service
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Mobileye is planning to enter the US robotaxi market.
The service will utilize its existing Moovit platform for deployment.
The planned launch in a US city is scheduled for 2027.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural networks (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim-Lim (1981), that image identity is encoded more in the phase than the magnitude of image representations.
The study uses a causal intervention method—transplanting phase between two images—to determine which input the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: TuneJury: An Open Metric for Improving Music Generation Preference Alignment
stories.author: Chris Donahue
bullet_points: Introduces TuneJury, an open, instance-level pairwise reward model for text-to-music generation.
The model predicts a music preference score based on a text prompt and an audio clip.
It is trained on diverse public human preference labels, including A vs. B votes and expert ratings.

stories.title: Bayesian Inference and Decision Audits for Public Archives of Frontier AI Evaluations
stories.author: Yanan Long
bullet_points: The paper critiques the common practice of viewing public AI evaluations as static leaderboards.
It proposes framing AI evaluation data (from LiveBench, Open LLM Leaderboard v2, etc.) as a Bayesian inference problem to account for reporting biases and missingness.
The research focuses on creating more robust, longitudinal records for frontier AI model assessment.

stories.title: When in Doubt, Plan It Out: Committed Small Language Model Deliberation for Reactive Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Odinaldo Rodrigues
bullet_points: Introduces PACT, a hybrid architecture designed to improve Reinforcement Learning (RL) robustness in unfamiliar environments.
Combines a fast, reactive RL policy with a slow, deliberative Small Language Model (SLM) planner for decision-making.
The SLM asynchronously generates and validates action plans, which are executed directly if deemed safe and feasible.

stories.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
stories.author: Sunghwan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) and World-Action Models (WAMs) by focusing on 3D geometry.
Proposes a 'Geometric Action Model' to enable generalist robot policies to reason about 3D physical interactions.
Aims to bridge the gap between semantic understanding (from large foundation models) and the geometric precision needed for contact-rich manipulation.

stories.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
stories.author: Hongsheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse binary outcomes (success/failure) in fine-tuning Vision-Language Agents (VLAs) using online Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Critiques existing methods that simplify sparse outcomes into a single scalar reward, which loses valuable transition-level feedback.
Proposes a novel 'Hierarchical Advantage Weighting' mechanism to provide richer, per-transition supervision during RL fine-tuning.

stories.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The research investigates whether modern neural network classifiers (PRISM2D, GFNet, ViT-B/16) exhibit the phase-sensitivity asymmetry observed in natural images.
It tests the hypothesis, based on Oppenheim and Lim (1981), that image identity is more encoded in the Fourier phase than in the magnitude.
The study uses a causal intervention technique—transplanting phase onto magnitude at a specific layer—to determine which input the classifier's prediction follows.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached states (KV cache).
Proposes 'KVEraser' to enable efficient, localized context editing without full recomputation.
Relevant for long-context LLM applications requiring correction of stale facts, retracted preferences, or prompt injections after prefill.

stories.title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training
stories.author: Aviral Kumar
bullet_points: Introduces ExpRL, a method for exploratory Reinforcement Learning (RL) applied during the mid-training phase of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses a limitation of current RL fine-tuning, which relies heavily on manually curated reasoning traces to teach primitive skills.
Aims to improve LLM reasoning by allowing the model to explore and learn skills more autonomously during training.

stories.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
stories.author: Jack Lindsey
bullet_points: Researchers investigated if language models internally track the 'value' of their current operational trajectory.
A 'value' axis was constructed for Qwen3-8B using synthetic in-context reinforcement learning data.
Activations along this axis correlate with model confidence, backtracking behavior, and code correctness.

stories.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of post-hoc context erasing in LLMs due to the global propagation of cached states (KV cache).
Proposes 'KVEraser,' a method to efficiently steer the KV cache for localized context deletion.
Relevant for long-context LLM applications where stale data, errors, or injections need to be removed after initial processing.

stories.title: DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Jiyan He
bullet_points: Introduces DEEPRUBRIC, a method using Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision to enhance Deep Research Agents.
The work addresses the inefficiency of current reinforcement learning approaches for research agents, which rely on rubric-based rewards.
It tackles the limitation where existing methods often rely on LLMs to generate rubrics, which can fail to capture the necessary task scope.

stories.title: IMPACTeen: Intentions, Manipulation, Persuasion, Annotations, and Consequences in Teen Communication Dataset
stories.author: Przemysław Kazienko
bullet_points: IMPACTeen is a novel dataset focusing on social influence, manipulation, and persuasion in adolescent communication.
The dataset features 1,021 texts and 5,100 annotations, uniquely labeled from five distinct expert perspectives (teenagers, parents, psychologists, etc.).
It was constructed using constrained LLM generation followed by rigorous human editing and validation, making it a valuable resource for NLP research.

stories.title: LESS Is More: Mutual-Stability Sampling for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Introduces LESS, a training-free method to improve sampling efficiency in Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs).
dLLMs are presented as a promising alternative to traditional autoregressive decoding due to parallel token updates.
The research addresses the inefficiency of current sampling procedures, which waste computation on stable or prematurely committed tokens.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: roman.pt via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The story concerns a security vulnerability or 'backdoor' found within a LinkedIn job offer.
This suggests a potential phishing, social engineering, or platform security issue.
The nature of the vulnerability is significant enough to warrant discussion in a tech community forum.

stories.title: BrowserAct vs Playwright: Where Test Automation Hits Real-World Anti-Bot Friction (Hands-On Comparison)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: Compares two automation tools, BrowserAct and Playwright, focusing on real-world anti-bot challenges.
Addresses the gap between successful local testing and failures in production/CI environments due to bot detection.
Provides a hands-on comparison relevant to QA engineers and automation practitioners.

stories.title: The $0 Bug That Cost Us $1,800 in API Calls
stories.author: arpitstack
bullet_points: A developer experienced a massive, unexplained spike in OpenAI API costs ($620 to $2,480 in 23 days).
The cost increase occurred without any corresponding new feature deployments, suggesting a hidden bug or inefficiency.
The incident highlights the financial risks and monitoring challenges associated with scaling LLM usage.
